Let n >= 6 be a positive integer. We call a positive integer n-Norwegian if it has three distinct positive divisors whose sum is equal to n. Let f(n) denote the smallest n-Norwegian positive integer. Let M = 3^(2025!) and for a non-negative integer c define

g(c) = (1 / 2025!) * floor((2025! * f(M + c)) / M).

We can write

g(0) + g(4M) + g(1848374) + g(10162574) + g(265710644) + g(44636594) = p / q,

where p and q are coprime positive integers. What is the remainder when p + q is divided by 99991?
